hero.title: Pub Marketing for Berkshire Pubs
hero.subtitle: Berkshire's 600 pubs span everything from affluent Windsor gastropubs to Reading commuter locals. We're based just over the border and know the M4 corridor market inside out.
hero.ctaText: Hi Peter, I run a pub in Berkshire and need help
hero.bottomText: £75/hour + VAT • From a licensee on the Berkshire border

intro.heading: What works in Berkshire (and what doesn't)
paragraphs: Berkshire splits into two markets: the affluent towns — Windsor, Ascot, Henley, Maidenhead — where food quality and positioning matter most, and the commuter belt — Reading, Bracknell, Wokingham — where convenience and midweek offers drive trade.

We run The Anchor in Stanwell Moor, right on the Berkshire border near the M4 and M25. We serve the same commuter crowd and compete for the same weekend trade. We know what works because we test it ourselves.

The biggest opportunity most Berkshire pubs miss? Seasonal events. Royal Ascot in June brings thousands of people looking for somewhere to eat and drink nearby. Reading Festival in late August does the same. If you're not marketing to those crowds in advance, you're leaving money on the table.

wins.heading: What's working for Berkshire pubs right now

items.title: Royal Ascot and event trade
items.description: Pubs within driving distance of Ascot Racecourse can fill every table during Royal Ascot week (June) with the right pre-event campaigns, packages, and Google positioning.

items.title: Win the gastropub battle
items.description: Berkshire's food scene is competitive. You don't need a Michelin star — you need brilliant photos, strong reviews, and a clear answer to 'why should I eat here instead of there?'

items.title: M4 corridor commuter trade
items.description: Reading, Bracknell, and Wokingham pubs can build reliable weekday lunch and after-work trade by targeting office park workers and station commuters with the right midweek offer.

items.title: Function and private hire
items.description: Affluent Berkshire means corporate events, birthday parties, and wedding receptions. Fix your booking process and Google visibility for 'function room near me' searches.

links.title: Start with pub marketing fundamentals
links.description: A practical overview and the best next page based on your biggest problem.

links.title: Build midweek momentum
links.description: Systems to build regulars and consistent Tuesday–Wednesday trade in the commuter belt.

links.title: Full services
links.description: Social, menus, events, training, and a simple 30-day plan tailored to your Berkshire pub.

faqs.question: Do you know the Berkshire pub market?
faqs.answer: Yes. We run The Anchor just over the border in Stanwell Moor, near the M4 and M25. We serve the same commuter belt, compete for weekend trade, and understand the mix of affluent destination dining and high-street locals that defines Berkshire.

faqs.question: Can you help us make the most of Royal Ascot week?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Royal Ascot brings huge footfall to pubs across Berkshire every June. We build pre-event campaigns, special menus, booking systems, and social content that fills your tables before, during, and after race week.

faqs.question: How do we compete with gastropubs in Windsor and Henley?
faqs.answer: Strong food photography, consistent reviews, and a clear 'why us' message. Most gastropubs rely on reputation and word of mouth — they're often weak on Google and social. That's your opportunity.

cta.title: Let's talk about your Berkshire pub
cta.subtitle: Tell me where your pub is and what's hurting trade. As a neighbouring licensee, I'll give you a straight answer on what to fix first.
cta.whatsappMessage: Hi Peter, I run a pub in Berkshire and I need help with marketing. Can we have a quick chat?
